sections.subtitle: Current Form
sections.text: 1. FC Köln have struggled recently, with no wins in their last 3 matches (1 draw, 2 losses), though they’ve shown resilience with late goals. FC St. Pauli, despite a recent win against Borussia M'gladbach (2-1), remain inconsistent, with just 2 wins in their last 5 matches. Köln's attacking output remains decent, while St. Pauli’s defense continues to leak goals, conceding in all 13 recent games.

sections.subtitle: Head-to-Head Record
sections.text: Historically, 1. FC Köln dominate this fixture, winning all 5 of their last encounters against FC St. Pauli, including a convincing 4-1 victory in 2019. Matches between these sides often produce high-scoring affairs, with over 2.5 goals in 4 of the last 5 head-to-heads. Köln have consistently been the first to score in recent meetings.

sections.subtitle: Season Statistics
sections.text: Köln, sitting 11th with 15 points, have scored 21 goals this season (6th in the league) but underperform their expected goals by -3.11. St. Pauli, languishing in 17th with just 7 points, have managed only 10 goals while conceding 24, highlighting defensive frailties. Köln’s superior big chance creation (34, 5th in league) gives them an edge over St. Pauli’s limited attacking threat.

sections.subtitle: Key Players to Watch
sections.text: For Köln, Jakub Kamiński and Said El Mala, both with 5 goals each, are in fine form and could exploit St. Pauli’s vulnerable defense. St. Pauli’s Danel Sinani offers some attacking hope with 2 goals, but missing key forward Andreas Hountondji may limit their firepower. Köln’s Ragnar Ache also has a strong record against St. Pauli with 2 goals in past matchups.

sections.subtitle: Match Prediction
sections.text: Given Köln’s historical dominance, better league position, and stronger attacking stats, they are clear favorites, aligning with Sofascore’s 63% home win probability. St. Pauli’s poor away form and defensive issues make an upset unlikely, though they might nick a goal. Expect a competitive match with Köln likely to secure the victory at RheinEnergieStadion.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Half-time overview
halftimeSections.text: The first half between 1. FC Köln and FC St. Pauli ended in a goalless draw, with neither side managing to break the deadlock. Köln showed more intent in attack, creating a couple of significant opportunities but failing to convert. St. Pauli, on the other hand, adopted a more defensive approach, focusing on maintaining structure while occasionally pushing forward. Both teams displayed moments of promise but lacked the cutting edge in the final third. The match remains finely poised as we head into the second half.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Home team performance
halftimeSections.text: 1. FC Köln controlled a larger share of possession in the first half and looked the more threatening side. They managed several entries into the penalty area and created notable chances, though their finishing let them down. Players like Luca Waldschmidt and Said El Mala showed glimpses of danger, but missed opportunities could haunt them if they don't capitalize in the second half. Defensively, Köln held firm, with no major lapses, though their duel-winning stats suggest they struggled in physical battles.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Away team performance
halftimeSections.text: FC St. Pauli put up a resilient display in the first half, focusing on defensive solidity over attacking ambition. They were dominant in physical duels, winning a high percentage of challenges, which helped disrupt Köln's rhythm. While they made frequent entries into the final third, they failed to create clear-cut chances, with their attacking output remaining minimal. Their goalkeeper, Nikola Vasilj, played a key role in keeping the score level with a strong performance.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Attack momentum analysis
halftimeSections.text: The first half saw fluctuating momentum, with Köln starting strongly and enjoying several prolonged spells of attacking pressure, particularly in the middle stages of the half. They consistently pushed forward and built dangerous moves, though without the final product. St. Pauli had their moments, especially towards the end of the half, where they gained traction and mounted pressure of their own. The momentum shifted back and forth, but Köln generally held the upper hand in terms of attacking intent.

halftimeSections.subtitle: Key areas to watch in the second half
halftimeSections.text: For Köln, the focus will be on converting their chances, as they’ve shown the ability to create openings but need a clinical finisher to step up. St. Pauli will likely look to exploit any frustration from the home side by maintaining their defensive organization and seeking counter-attacking opportunities. The midfield battle will be crucial, as both teams aim to control the tempo. Set pieces could also play a decisive role, given St. Pauli’s physical dominance and Köln’s need for a breakthrough.
